reasoning: The current value of the 4-week moving average is 242,250, which is relatively high for the series and represents a local peak. The FRED trend analysis gives a combined 30% probability of an increase for the near-term based on mean reversion and seasonal patterns. For the first resolution date (June 29), the replacement week needs to exceed a very high 244,000 mark for the average to go up, which is unlikely given recent momentum and holiday effects slowing claims. Further out (July and September), historical seasonality usually pulls jobless claims lower after the mid-summer auto shutdowns, leading to strong baseline expectations that the value will be below 242,250. I have set the probabilities to gently approach 0.5 for the later dates to correctly reflect increasing overall variance and macro uncertainty over a wider forecast horizon.
